LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE OCT-24-2024 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., RASIER, LLC, AND RASIER-CA, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS' (UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., RASIER, LLC, RASIER-CA, LLC) MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY IS GRANTED (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING, WITH A PROPOSED ORDER STATING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Oct-24-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Apperances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. COURTNEY WERT, Esq. of McNamara Law Firm (tel: 707-920-2202) for Defendant Leonard Steinberg.
No appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., RASIER, LLC, AND RASIER-CA, LLC's MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION.

Matter not argued/contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' (UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., RASIER, LLC, RASIER-CA, LLC) motion to compel arbitration and stay is granted.

"The petitioner bears the burden of proving the existence of a valid arbitration agreement by the preponderance of the evidence, and a party opposing the petition bears the burden of proving by a preponderance of the evidence any fact necessary to its defense." (Engalla v. Permanente Medical Group, Inc. (1997) 15 Cal.4th 951, 972.)

Defendants establish that plaintiff assented to defendants' terms of service, which include the agreement to arbitrate and a clear and unmistakable delegation clause. (Gamboa v. Northeast Community Clinic (2021) 72 Cal.App.5th 158 [explaining burden necessary to establish an agreement to arbitrate].) The Gaddis declaration demonstrates that plaintiff agreed to arbitrate under the April 4, 2022, terms of service. Plaintiff clicked a button agreeing to those terms. (Gaddis Decl., pars. 9-13.) "Under Civil Code section 1633.7, ... an electronic signature has the same legal effect as a handwritten signature." (Ruiz v. Moss Bros. Auto Group, Inc. (2014) 232 Cal.App.4th 836, 843; Nguyen v. Barnes & Noble Inc. (9th Cir. 2014) 763 F.3d 1171, 1176-1177[distinguishing between clickwrap and browsewrap agreements and noting that clickwrap agreements, which require affirmative acknowledgement, are generally enforceable].) Plaintiff does not introduce any controverting evidence and fails to show any defense to the delegation clause itself. Therefore, the case is ordered to arbitration. (Rent-A-Center, W., Inc. v. Jackson (2010) 561 U.S. 63, 70-71 [delegation clause severable]; Malone v. Superior Court (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 1551, 1560 ["the court must determine whether the delegation clause itself may be enforced (and can only delegate the general issue of enforceability to the arbitrator if it first determines the delegation clause is enforceable)."].)

The parties agreed that the Federal Arbitration Act ("FAA") governs the parties' interpretation and enforcement of the agreement. (Gaddis Decl., Ex. C [Terms of Service, par. 2(c).) Accordingly, plaintiff cannot rely on CCP 1281.2(c) to avoid arbitration. (Victrola 89, LLC v. Jaman Properties 8 LLC (2020) 46 Cal.App.5th 337, 355 [explaining FAA preemption]; Rodriguez v. American Technologies, Inc. (2006) 136 Cal.App.4th 1110, 1122.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, with a proposed order stating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)
